title: Respect one-move windows instead of trying to take three actions from one turn
body: A lot of early jungle mistakes come from trying to fit too many actions into a window that only supports one clean answer. If the enemy jungler spends their turn on grubs, your response might only have room for one real move: dragon, invade, or gank. Trying to stack all three often means the board updates before you finish and the last action becomes a tempo loss or a death.
actionables: When the enemy shows, ask what your single best answer is before chaining extra actions onto it.
Treat early windows like one-turn exchanges unless lane priority or huge lead clearly gives you more.
Review failed sequences by marking the first good action and the extra action that made the sequence collapse.

source.title: 1 Year of Jungle Coaching in 1hr 26mins | 2025 Fundamentals Guide
source.notes: Built from Leo's turn-based tempo framing: one enemy move usually allows one clean answer before the map updates.
